text.source_text: अतिमात्रस्यापि परमेश्वरस्य प्रादेशमात्रत्वमभिव्यक्तिनिमित्तं स्यात् । अभिव्यज्यते किल प्रादेशमात्रपरिमाणः परमेश्वर उपासकानां कृते । प्रदेशविशेषेषु वा हृदयादिषूपलब्धिस्थानेषु विशेषेणाभिव्यज्यते । अतः परमेश्वरेऽपि प्रादेशमात्रश्रुतिरभिव्यक्तेरुपपद्यत इत्याश्मरथ्य आचार्यो मन्यते ॥ २९ ॥
text.transliteration_iast: atimātrasyāpi parameśvarasya prādeśamātratvamabhivyaktinimittaṃ syāt | abhivyajyate kila prādeśamātraparimāṇaḥ parameśvara upāsakānāṃ kṛte | pradeśaviśeṣeṣu vā hṛdayādiṣūpalabdhisthāneṣu viśeṣeṇābhivyajyate | ataḥ parameśvare'pi prādeśamātraśrutirabhivyakterupapadyata ityāśmarathya ācāryo manyate || 29 ||

translation.literal_translation: Even though the Supreme Lord is measureless, the 'span-sized' description may be due to manifestation. The Supreme, though vast, is manifested for worshipers as if of span-measure; or He is especially manifested in specific loci of apprehension such as the heart. Therefore even for the Supreme, the sruti of pradesa-matra (span-measure) is intelligible through manifestation - so Acarya Asmarathya holds. (29)
translation.technical_translation: Bhashya on 1.2.29 (Asmarathya) explains pradesa-matra as abhivyakti-nimitta: finite measure-language tracks manifested accessibility, not intrinsic limitation of Brahman.
translation.interpretive_note: Localization vocabulary is treated as phenomenological disclosure, not ontological size.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Tracks each block inside the section-level inferential sequence

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S02 pass with stable cadence and inferential-tag schema.
A later philological pass should revisit pradesa-matra, sampatti, and recensional lexical correspondences in records 1.2.071-1.2.078.
